title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 10th February 2026 | TV5 News

recommendations.note: Hold; near 1000 now, registrar business growing with new IPOs; can reach 1175-1200 by Mar-Apr.
recommendations.detail: For a viewer holding at 1089 (at a loss), he says hold and don't panic. KFin is near 1000 now (986) and will easily cross back up. As brokerage/registrar businesses grow and new IPOs keep coming, KFin will definitely improve. It can go up to 1175-1200 by next March-April.

recommendations.note: Hold but exit on bounce; narrow band 300-340, no immediate move to old 400+ highs.
recommendations.detail: The worst is likely over but the stock moves in a narrow band between 300 and 340. The viewer bought at 340; that price will come easily and when it does, he says exit. He does not expect the old highs of 400-plus to come immediately.

recommendations.note: Hold; stable depository business, no competition (only NSDL and CDSL).
recommendations.detail: For a viewer holding at 1493 (at a loss), he says hold. Whatever IPO rally happens, depository companies have a stable business with no competition - only NSDL and CDSL exist. Even without steady growth it is a steady business model; once costs are cut and profits shown better, there is scope for the stock to rise.

recommendations.note: Safest route if you can't bear individual-stock volatility (vs large-caps).
recommendations.detail: Asked about replacing individual large-cap stocks with a Nifty ETF, he says ETF is a safe play. If you are in individual stocks and cannot bear their ups, downs and volatility, a Nifty ETF is the safest route - no doubt about it.

recommendations.note: Hold, don't shift to Avantel yet; check result, may go 200+ after March then shift.
recommendations.detail: Holder of 200 free shares asked about switching to Avantel. He says check the result first - Lokesh was hit indirectly by American tariffs and some sanctions, seen in the stock. The best of times are over and it corrected to 200, but after March there are signs of it going to 200-plus; shift at that time, not now.

recommendations.note: Not cheap; high-PE stock already near 154-155; don't shift into it now.
recommendations.detail: On switching from Lokesh Machines into Avantel, he says Avantel is not a cheap stock even now. By PE-wise it is a high-PE stock and is already trading near 154-155. So don't shift right now from Lokesh into Avantel.

recommendations.note: Switch from PPL Pharma to Laurus Labs - no doubt.
recommendations.detail: The viewer is at about a 15% loss in PPL Pharma and asked about switching to Laurus. He says from PPL to Laurus you can shift, no doubt about it.

recommendations.note: Switch target from PPL Pharma - no doubt.
recommendations.detail: He endorses shifting from PPL Pharma into Laurus, saying there is no doubt about that switch.

recommendations.note: Hold; first post-name-change quarterly given optimistically; good long-term returns.
recommendations.detail: Hold Sequent Scientific. After the name change, the first conference was just the other day - this is the first-quarter result, given optimistically. The earnings-call transcript has to be seen, but in the long term it can give good returns.

On Rekha's mutual-fund SIPs (Nippon India Small Cap, Kotak Flexi Cap, HDFC Large & Mid, Mirae Asset Large & Mid), he says they are fine - all performing nicely. Continue the SIPs as they give good returns in the long run.
Part of Rekha's SIP set that he endorses continuing - all are performing nicely and give good long-run returns.

recommendations.stock: HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund
recommendations.note: Continue SIP; sometimes underperforms ICICI Pru/Motilal Oswal Large&Mid but good long-run.
recommendations.detail: He says HDFC Large & Mid (and Mirae Asset) sometimes underperform compared to ICICI Prudential or Motilal Oswal Large & Mid, but otherwise in the long run they give good returns; continue the SIP.

recommendations.stock: Mirae Asset Large and Mid Cap Fund
recommendations.note: Continue SIP; sometimes underperforms peers but good long-run returns.
recommendations.detail: Part of Rekha's SIP set; sometimes underperforms ICICI Prudential or Motilal Oswal Large & Mid, but otherwise gives good long-run returns - continue.

recommendations.note: Buy; ~13% dividend, parent toll revenue up 6500->7500 cr YoY; five toll assets + a HAM asset.
recommendations.detail: Definitely buy IRB InvIT - no need to fear. The parent IRB Infra's toll revenues are strong and factorisation benefit accrues to this trust; toll revenue jumped year-on-year from 6,500 crore to 7,500 crore. It shows ~13% dividend, is near its 52-week high, and has five toll assets in Western and Southern India plus a HAM asset - looks stronger.

recommendations.note: Hold if you have the time horizon; undervalued, deploys cash into equities; else switch to MF.
recommendations.detail: Maithan Alloys is an undervalued company that deploys its cash into listed/unlisted equities rather than core business (it bought ~40,000-50,000 NSE shares at ~1,000, now doubled). If you have the time horizon, invest/hold; otherwise there are much better companies and a mutual fund is the better route.

recommendations.note: Hold and wait for the 12th board-meeting (Dec) result; turnaround would help, else may retest 52-wk low.
recommendations.detail: For a viewer at 352 now at a 50% loss, he says it disappointed since its hyped listing - even the September result was disappointing. There is a board meeting on the 12th for the December-quarter result; if a turnaround shows, the stock improves, otherwise it may retest the 52-week low. Having waited this long, it is better to hold and see the result.

recommendations.note: Hold, don't book profit; group flagship in capital markets, near 52-week high - keep a trailing stop and let it run.
recommendations.detail: For a viewer at average 120, he says hold and don't book profit. It is the group's flagship company in the capital markets and the stock is near its 52-week high. In stocks touching 52-week highs you should keep a trading stop, protect profits and let it run rather than selling just because it has risen.

recommendations.note: Hold long-term; good margins, US-India trade deal a positive; range-bound with short/medium-term global impacts.
recommendations.detail: For a viewer holding from IPO time, he says hold. The US-India trade deal is a positive and the stock shows traction, but it is range-bound. A company with good margins; global impacts weigh in the medium-to-short term, but it is a stock to hold on for the long term.
